summary: Verified the new Customer Returns 'return_type' feature end-to-end. Backend: 5/5 pytest tests pass — RETURNED approve→Credit Note (CN-...), MISSING approve→Debit Note (DN-YYYY-####) written to db.debit_notes with original==balance==total_credit, status='pending'; return doc reflects only debit_note_id/_number (no credit_note_id); default+invalid return_type fall back to 'returned'; re-approve is rejected (only draft → approved) so create_debit_note_from_return is effectively idempotent. Frontend: dialog title 'Track Customer Return', both choice cards present (return-type-returned default-emerald w/ 'Credit Note' advisory, return-type-missing amber w/ 'Debit Note' advisory), submit label toggles between 'Track Return (Credit Note)' and 'Track Missing (Debit Note)', note-badge-{id} renders CN-* (emerald) on existing returned rows.
tests_executed: Playwright: dialog title 'Track Customer Return' confirmed
Playwright: return-type-missing selects (amber-500 in class) and submit label flips to 'Track Missing (Debit Note)'
Playwright: toggling back restores 'Track Return (Credit Note)'
Playwright: existing CN- badges render via [data-testid=note-badge-{id}]
critical_code_review_comments: routes/customer_returns.py: approve endpoint does not require role check (only 'admins approve' is enforced via UI). Confirm RBAC at backend matches spec — at present any authenticated user with route access can call /approve. If CEO-only is required server-side, add a role guard.

calculate_item_credit ignores return_type — for 'missing' you still compute credit using sku_return_credit / full_price / percentage. This is intentional per spec ('debit amount = same total_credit') but worth a code comment so future maintainers do not 'fix' it.

create_debit_note_from_return idempotency works on the document level (early-return when existing), but the second /approve call now returns 400 before reaching it — so the idempotency guard is currently dead code. Either keep it as defense-in-depth (fine) or expose a re-issue endpoint that uses it.

Debit notes are stored in the same db.debit_notes collection as credit-note records share (per generate_debit_note_number regex on debit_note_number). Make sure no existing collection users assume note_type='credit' only — quick grep recommended.
ReturnsTab.jsx is large (>1000 lines) — splitting the Track-Return Dialog into its own component would help.
test_credentials: CEO surya.yadavalli@nylaairwater.earth / test123 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md). Distributor used: Brian DIST-0003 (id bb12d90e-4d33-4890-ac5f-17573c551b5c).
seed_data_creation: Backend tests created ~5 customer_returns (mix of returned/missing) for Brian and approved 4 of them. All created returns were deleted in fixture teardown; the linked debit_notes documents are NOT auto-cleaned (delete endpoint only cancels credit_notes). Operator may want to purge db.debit_notes where note_type='debit' and return_number not in db.customer_returns to keep dev data tidy.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Distributor Returns tab URL: /distributors/{id}?tab=returns (do NOT click the sidebar 'Customer Returns' — it navigates to global sales module page). Key selectors: create-return-btn, return-type-returned, return-type-missing, submit-track-return-btn, note-badge-{return_id}. Backend endpoints: POST/GET /api/distributors/{distributor_id}/returns, POST .../{return_id}/approve returns {credit_note, debit_note} where exactly one is non-null. For total_credit>0 use an account whose sku_pricing entry has return_bottle_credit>0 plus a return_reason with credit_type='sku_return_credit'.
